channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: I asked ChatGPT and Gemini how to make French Toast as good as my mother used to make — one nailed the crispy-sweet finish
item.description: Gemini or ChatGPT can help you make your favorite food, as long as you give it the right kind of prompt.

item.title: Ilya Sutskever Stands by His Role in Sam Altman’s OpenAI Ouster: ‘I Didn’t Want It to Be Destroyed’
item.description: The former OpenAI chief scientist may be estranged from the company, but he still came to its defense as he testified on Monday.

item.title: OpenAI just released its answer to Claude Mythos
item.description: OpenAI is launching Daybreak, an AI initiative focused on detecting and patching vulnerabilities before attackers find them. Daybreak uses the Codex Security AI agent that launched in March to create a threat model based on an organization's code and focus on possible attack paths, validate likely vulnerabilities, and then automate the detection of the higher […]...

item.title: GM just laid off hundreds of IT workers to hire those with stronger AI skills
item.description: Some of the positions focus on AI-native development, data engineering and analytics, cloud-based engineering, and agent and model development as well as prompt engineering and new AI workflows.

item.title: Quordle hints and answers for Tuesday, May 12 (game #1569)
item.description: Looking for Quordle clues? We can help. Plus get the answers to Quordle today and past solutions.

item.title: NYT Connections hints and answers for Tuesday, May 12 (game #1066)
item.description: Looking for NYT Connections answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, plus my commentary on the puzzles.

item.title: NYT Strands hints and answers for Tuesday, May 12 (game #800)
item.description: Looking for NYT Strands answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, including the spangram.

item.title: One leaker claims Apple's long-rumored plans for Touch ID on Apple Watch have been shelved in favor of 'packing in larger batteries' — and as a Garmin main who's recently switched, that's totally fine by me
item.description: Apple is allegedly shelving plans to add Touch ID to Apple Watches — instead, they're focusing on something much more important, according to leaks

item.title: Astronomers use the Webb telescope to improve our map of the cosmic web
item.description: Adding significantly more detail means better future investigations into the universe's past.

item.title: Yarbo says it will remove the intentional backdoor from its robot lawn mower
item.description: The company behind the robot lawn mower that ran me over has changed its tune. Yarbo now plans to completely remove the remote backdoor access that could have let bad actors reprogram the robot over the internet. Yarbo customers will be able to decide whether that feature even gets installed in the first place, co-founder […]

item.title: Nvidia GeForce NOW data breach confirmed — but luckily most of us will be safe, here's why
item.description: The breach is limited to GeForce NOW users in just one country, and no passwords were stolen.

item.title: An AI agent runs this experimental Swedish café. Here’s how it’s going
item.description: San Francisco-based startup Andon Labs has put ‘Mona’ in charge at its eponymous café in Stockholm. The coffee might be poured by a human hand, but behind the counter, something far less traditional is calling the shots at an experimental café in Stockholm.

item.title: Thinking Machines shows off preview of near-realtime AI voice and video conversation with new 'interaction models'
item.description: Is AI leaving the era of "turn-based" chat?Right now, all of us who use AI models regularly for work or in our personal lives know that the basic interaction mode across text, imagery, audio, and video remains the same: the human user provides an input, waits anywhere between milliseconds to minutes (or in some cases, for particularly tough queries, hours and days), and the AI model provides an output.But if AI is to really take on the load of jobs requiring natural interaction, it will need to do more than provide this kind of "turn-based" interactivity — it will ultimately need to respond mo...

item.title: Here’s what Mira Murati’s AI company is up to
item.description: Thinking Machines, the AI company founded by former OpenAI CTO Mira Murati, announced Monday that it's working on something called "interaction models." The idea behind interaction models, according to Thinking Machines, is that they will let people "collaborate with AI the way we naturally collaborate with each other - they continuously take in audio, video, […]...

item.title: We dont need content cops on social media. We need better design.
item.description: Online activist Lennon Torres says Meta's clumsy speech guidelines mask a bigger problem — addictive and dangerous design.
